key_departures.impact: Released after only 12 games across two injury-plagued seasons. Originally signed to a 4yr/$76.4M deal with $44M guaranteed after a trade with Kansas City. Zero INTs in Tennessee, placed on IR both seasons. Off-field legal issues compounded the bust. Saves $11.4M in 2026 cap space with $8.1M dead cap charge.
Primary receiving TE and best offensive playmaker at the position. Departure leaves a genuine hole at receiving TE alongside second-year Gunnar Helm.
Rotational pass rusher who was never expected back. Departs to division rival Colts. Exacerbates an already thin EDGE room.
Starting right guard for Tennessee. Veteran presence and stabilizing force alongside JC Latham. Departure opens a starting guard spot.
Released in late February. Was the starting center. Released before free agency opened, directly creating a center need.
Interior depth piece. Departure thins the DL rotation behind Jeffery Simmons.

Traded to Jets in exchange for EDGE Jermaine Johnson. Was a starting nose tackle and 2024 second-round pick. Created a void at IDL that the team addressed with Franklin-Myers and Elliott. Net positive trade despite the IDL loss.
Released in late February. Was a starting safety. Departure creates a safety need, though Kevin Winston Jr. (2025 3rd-round pick) is expected to develop into a starter.
Veteran WR who contributed minimally in 2025. Age and injury history made departure expected. No significant need escalation given his limited role.
Was traded away during the 2025 season. Listed for completeness. Already absorbed into prior depth chart.

signings.analysis: Three-year deal at $20M AAV with $42M fully guaranteed. Elite-tier contract for the CB market. Was a four-year starter with the Saints, can play outside or nickel. $42M in full guarantees signals a true investment — this is their CB1. Pairs with Flott to form a legitimate starting CB duo. Robert Saleh's press-heavy scheme gets a cornerstone piece.

Three-year/$45M deal with $32M fully guaranteed. High-end starter-tier contract. Started 14 games for the Giants in 2025 with 11 pass deflections and 1 INT. Long, athletic corner familiar with multiple coverage schemes via Daboll. Combined with Taylor, the Titans spent $105M on their CB room in a single day, transforming the biggest pre-FA need.

Two-year depth signing. Played primarily on special teams with the Chiefs (255 ST snaps), saw limited defensive action (15%). Will compete for a roster spot. Need at CB already addressed by Taylor and Flott. This is a depth/ST add, not a starter investment. Borgonzi familiarity play from his Chiefs days.

Three-year/$63M deal with $42M guaranteed — top-of-market for interior DL. Franklin-Myers had 7.5 sacks and 15 QB hits in 2025. He fills the void created by the T'Vondre Sweat trade and plugs in next to Jeffery Simmons. Reunites with Robert Saleh, under whom he thrived with the Jets. Three years of real guarantees signal this is a foundational piece. IDL need addressed.

Acquired via trade (T'Vondre Sweat for Johnson, straight swap). Johnson tallied 13 sacks, 27 QB hits, and 20 TFLs across 47 career games with the Jets. He is on the final year(s) of his rookie deal, making him a controlled-cost starter. Reunites with Saleh. Partially addresses EDGE given the contract situation is short-term; Titans still need to invest in EDGE depth or a long-term solution. EDGE moves from critical to moderate need — draft investment still likely.

Two-year depth deal. Career 26.5 sacks across 119 games. Smaller, quicker EDGE who fits Saleh's upfield-attack scheme. Provides rotational depth behind Johnson and Simmons but does not solve the long-term EDGE starter problem. EDGE remains a Tier 2 draft need.

One-year prove-it deal. Big-bodied end who has missed significant time in his career. Known to GM Borgonzi from their shared time in Kansas City. Camp competition and rotational depth only. Does not move the needle on the EDGE need.

IDL need already addressed by Franklin-Myers. Elliott is a depth addition who started 16 games for the 49ers in Saleh's system in 2025. Two-year/$8M deal is solidly below the starter threshold. Adds rotational depth and familiar scheme fit behind Simmons and Franklin-Myers.

Acquired in a seventh-round pick swap from the Cowboys (TEN receives pick 225, sends 218). Veteran depth piece. Former Jets player under Saleh. Provides interior DL rotation depth only — IDL need already addressed.

Four-year/$70M deal with ~$40M guaranteed. Record contract for a slot receiver. Robinson had 92 catches for 1,014 yards and 4 TDs with the Giants in 2025 under Daboll. He instantly becomes Cam Ward's WR1. The guaranteed structure ($38M base in first two years) signals a real 3-4 year commitment. WR need drops significantly — still thin depth behind him, but the starter question is answered.

Three-year/$24M deal at $8M AAV — high-end of the TE starter range but not elite. In-line blocking TE who reunites with Daboll; accumulated 88 catches/934 yards in four seasons with the Giants. Pairs with Gunnar Helm. Okonkwo was a receiving specialist and that role isn't fully replicated. Partially addresses TE — the team still lacks a premium receiving TE with Okonkwo's quickness. Draft investment at TE still possible.

Two-year depth deal. Move TE who provides some of the receiving ability that Okonkwo had — Saleh's coaching staff noted he fills a similar receiving role. Had 368 receiving yards for the Colts in 2023. Does not elevate TE from a need position but adds useful depth.

Two-year/$9M deal with only $3.5M guaranteed. Expected to compete for the starting center job after Lloyd Cushenberry was released. Made four starts for the Giants in 2025, is familiar with Daboll's system. However, the low guarantee structure and modest AAV signal a bridge/competition role, not a locked-in solution. IOL need (specifically C) remains elevated — team was priced out of top options like Linderbaum. Draft investment at C very likely.

One-year/$4.215M deal (max value) with $2.5M guaranteed. One-year structure is a pure bridge/competition signing. Has 48 career starts at guard for the Bengals. Adds interior line depth and competition but does not solve the starting guard vacancy left by Kevin Zeitler. IOL remains a Tier 2 need, especially at center and right guard.

Two-year backup deal. Reunites with offensive coordinator Daboll from their time in Buffalo in 2021. Provides a veteran backup for second-year QB Cam Ward. Low-cost, low-risk move. Effectively signals Will Levis' departure from the roster. No need impact at QB — Ward is the clear starter.

One-year deal. Started 36 games with the Jets under Saleh. Reuniting with a familiar coaching staff. Will compete with Amani Hooker and 2025 third-round pick Kevin Winston Jr. One-year structure signals depth/competition, not a solution. S remains a moderate need given the Xavier Woods release.
Re-signed to a one-year deal after being claimed off waivers from the Seahawks last fall. Played 3 games for the Titans in 2025. Depth-only signing. No impact on S need.

Two-year/$6M deal. Two-time AFC Special Teams Player of the Year-caliber punter. First-team All-Pro in 2022. Replaces Johnny Hekker. Special teams upgrade but no impact on positional needs tracked in this pipeline.
Re-signed on a one-year deal. Made 80% of field goals in 2025 including 9-of-14 from 50+. Continuity at the kicker position. No impact on tracked positional needs.
One-year re-sign. One of the best long snappers in the league. Provides continuity for the kicking game. No impact on tracked positional needs.

Exclusive rights tender. Third-year receiver who missed most of 2025 with a knee injury. Special teams contributor. No impact on the WR starting depth — Robinson is WR1 and Calvin Ridley (restructured) remains on the roster.

Contract restructured to keep him in Nashville. Caught 17 passes for 303 yards in 7 games in 2025 before a broken fibula ended his season. Provides veteran WR depth alongside Robinson. The restructure signal is a one-year bridge — WR depth beyond 2026 may still be addressed in the draft.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Titans spent $105M on two starting cornerbacks (Taylor 3yr/$60M + Flott 3yr/$45M), both with substantial full guarantees. CB was the No. 1 pre-FA need and has been comprehensively overhauled. Williams adds depth. CB should now be a luxury/camp competition pick only in the draft.

Franklin-Myers (3yr/$63M, $42M guaranteed) is a proven starter pairing with Jeffery Simmons. Elliott adds depth. While T'Vondre Sweat was traded away, the net result is a more complete IDL room than before. IDL is no longer a draft need of consequence.

Robinson (4yr/$70M, ~$40M guaranteed, record slot deal) immediately becomes the WR1. Ridley was restructured and stays as the WR2. Oliver returns on ERFA tender. The Titans went from WR-needy to adequately stocked. WR is no longer a top-5 pick-priority need.

Johnson trade is a positive addition (13 career sacks, Saleh familiarity) but he is on a short-term deal with no guaranteed big contract extension yet confirmed. Martin and Herring are depth pieces only. Arden Key left. EDGE still needs a long-term investment — this is still a strong draft priority at picks 35 or 66.

Schlottmann (2yr/$9M, $3.5M guaranteed) is a bridge center, not a solution — the Titans were priced out of top centers. Volson (1yr/$4.2M) is IOL depth/competition. Zeitler and Cushenberry departed. Two starting spots are in flux. Strong IOL need remains — center in particular is still a priority for rounds 2-4 of the draft.

Bellinger (3yr/$24M) is a quality in-line blocker who reunites with Daboll, and Granson adds receiving depth. However, neither fully replicates Okonkwo's receiving impact from the slot. Helm is young. TE remains a moderate need — rounds 3-5 consideration.

Xavier Woods was released. Adams is a one-year depth bridge. Winston Jr. (2025 3rd-round pick) is the developmental option. Amani Hooker is retained. S need is moderate — depth is thin but the Kevin Winston development path provides some comfort. Day 3 draft add likely.
No OT moves made in free agency. JC Latham holds down one side. Need on the other side remains unchanged from pre-FA assessment.
No RB moves made. Multiple analyst projections suggest the Titans are building toward a RB selection at No. 4 overall (Jeremiyah Love). Need level is unchanged heading into draft.
No significant LB signings. Demario Davis (2yr/$22M) went elsewhere. LB depth remains thin for Saleh's defense. Draft or later FA addition likely.

key_additions.headline: Physical, blitz-capable CB1 anchors a completely rebuilt secondary under Robert Saleh
Starting CB2 with 11 PDs in 2025 pairs with Taylor to give Titans their best CB room in years
Record-setting slot WR (1,014 yards in 2025) becomes Cam Ward's top target in Daboll's system
7.5-sack interior pass rusher reunites with Saleh, forms elite DL pairing with Jeffery Simmons

key_additions.contract_summary: Acquired via trade (T'Vondre Sweat to Jets)
key_additions.headline: Former 26th overall pick with 13 career sacks brings immediate EDGE impact for Saleh's defense
In-line TE reunites with Daboll, upgrades Tennessee's run-blocking and 12-personnel attack
Bridge center with Daboll system familiarity steps in to compete for starting job vacated by Cushenberry
Veteran backup reunites with OC Daboll to mentor and support second-year starter Cam Ward
First-team All-Pro punter upgrades Tennessee's field position game, replacing Johnny Hekker

key_losses.headline: Bust-level investment ends after only 12 games across two injury-plagued seasons in Tennessee
Primary receiving TE and most dynamic offensive weapon at the position departs after 4 seasons
2024 second-round NT traded to Jets in value-positive swap that brought EDGE Johnson to Nashville
Starting right guard and veteran leader departs, leaving another hole on an unsettled interior line
Rotational pass rusher joins division-rival Colts, thinning an already undermanned EDGE room
Starting center released in cap move, opening up the most critical unaddressed spot on the offensive line
